headline: Core ERC-20 contract verified and audited, but deployment is years old, custodian structure changed significantly in 2024 (BitGo/BiT Global transition), and no re-audit of the current operational model is publicly documented
short_headline: Verified; audits stale post-2024 custody shift

findings.text: The WBTC ERC-20 token contract at 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 on Ethereum is fully verified on Etherscan (Contract Source Code Verified badge present). It is a non-proxy, monolithic contract (no separate implementation address needed). The WBTC DAO multisig and minting/burning controller contracts are also present on-chain, though their Etherscan verification status could only be partially confirmed within this run (see unknowns).

The GitHub organisation WrappedBTC hosts https://github.com/WrappedBTC/bitcoin-token-smart-contracts which contains the Solidity source for the WBTC token. The Etherscan-visible source for 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 corresponds to the WBTCToken.sol / ERC20.sol structure in that repo. A precise commit-SHA match was not pinned in this run (see unknowns V2).

The audit dashboard at https://wbtc.network/dashboard/audit lists audits by Trail of Bits (August 2018) and Solidified (2018). These audits cover the original ERC-20 contract and the minting/burning controller. Both audits are now more than 6 years old. No audit covering the 2024 custodian transition (BitGo to BiT Global / Justin Sun involvement) or the updated multi-institution DAO structure is listed on the public dashboard.

Trail of Bits is a broadly recognised Solidity/security auditing firm (explicitly named in the rubric's recognised list). Solidified was a recognised smart-contract auditing platform (now largely absorbed / wound down). The original audits are from credible firms.

Post-audit drift: The core ERC-20 contract bytecode has not changed since the 2018 deployment (it is not upgradeable), so the audited code still matches what is deployed for the token contract itself. However, the broader WBTC system—merchant/custodian registry, controller contract, and the DAO multisig governance layer—has undergone significant operational changes in 2024 (custodian transition to BiT Global). No public re-audit of the controller or DAO multisig contracts reflecting these changes is documented on the audit dashboard. This constitutes meaningful post-audit drift at the system level even though the token contract itself is unchanged.

The WBTC token at 0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599 is not a proxy contract—it is a directly deployed implementation. There is no separate proxy/implementation split to verify. The controller contract (0xCA06411bd7a7296d7e0ae9b29f09638df5e86a57) and DAO multisig addresses also appear to be direct contracts, not proxies, though full verification of each auxiliary contract was not completed in this run.

steelman.red: The only publicly listed audits date from 2018 and do not cover the material 2024 custodian-transition operational changes or the current DAO multisig composition, effectively meaning the live system has had no credible audit in over six years.
steelman.orange: The core ERC-20 contract is verified on Etherscan and was audited by Trail of Bits (a top-tier firm), but the audits are stale (2018), the custodian structure changed materially in 2024 without a documented re-audit, and auxiliary contracts' verification status was not fully confirmed in this run.
steelman.green: The WBTC ERC-20 token contract is immutable, source-verified on Etherscan, corresponds to a public GitHub repo, and was audited by Trail of Bits; because the contract bytecode itself has not changed, the audited code is precisely what runs today.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because while the immutable ERC-20 token contract is verified on Etherscan and was audited by Trail of Bits in 2018, the broader WBTC system (controller, DAO multisig, custodian registry) underwent a material operational change in 2024 (custodian transition to BiT Global/Justin Sun) with no publicly documented re-audit on the audit dashboard, representing significant post-audit drift at the system level (V5). Additionally, auxiliary contract verification was not fully confirmed (V1 partial). The green steel-man is undercut by the 6+ year audit gap and the unaudited custody model change; the red steel-man is too strong because the core token contract itself remains verified and immutable against its Trail of Bits-audited codebase.

evidence.shows: WBTC ERC-20 token contract on Ethereum — source code verified on Etherscan, non-proxy direct deployment
Official WrappedBTC GitHub repository containing WBTC ERC-20 Solidity source code that corresponds to the Etherscan-verified contract
WBTC public audit dashboard listing Trail of Bits (August 2018) and Solidified (2018) audits as the only published audits; no post-2018 or post-2024-custodian-change audits listed
Trail of Bits is a recognised smart-contract and blockchain security auditing firm (referenced for V4 auditor recognition)
WBTC Controller contract on Ethereum — auxiliary contract governing minting/burning, used to confirm non-proxy structure
Reports the 2024 WBTC custodian transition from BitGo to BiT Global (Justin Sun involvement), a material operational change post-dating all published audits

unknowns: V2: A precise commit SHA matching the Etherscan-verified WBTC token source to the WrappedBTC/bitcoin-token-smart-contracts GitHub repo was not pinned in this run; a visual/structural correspondence was observed but no bytecode diff or compiler-artifact match was performed.

V1: Etherscan verification status of auxiliary contracts (DAO multisig, merchant registry) beyond the main token contract (0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599) and the controller (0xCA06411bd7a7296d7e0ae9b29f09638df5e86a57) was not fully confirmed for all addresses in the WBTC system.

V3: No audit report PDF from the Trail of Bits 2018 WBTC audit was directly retrieved and read in this run to confirm exact contract scope and commit SHA; the audit listing was observed on the dashboard page only.
V5: The specific controller contract version deployed post-2024 custodian transition and whether it was audited by any firm not listed on the public dashboard is unknown.

protocol_metadata.about: WBTC (Wrapped Bitcoin) is an ERC-20 token on Ethereum that represents Bitcoin at a 1:1 ratio, allowing BTC holders to use their assets in Ethereum DeFi applications. Users deposit BTC with a custodian (currently BiT Global, formerly BitGo), which mints an equivalent amount of WBTC on Ethereum; redemption burns WBTC and releases the underlying BTC. The system is governed by a DAO of merchants and custodians who control the minting/burning controller contract via multisig. WBTC was the first and remains the largest tokenised Bitcoin on Ethereum by market capitalisation.
